library.description: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3: النوافذ، الإدخال، الأحداث، الصوت، والرسم الأساسي.

kind.description: استدعاءات تنفذ فعلاً، تغيّر حالة، أو تعيد نتيجة، أو تبني مورداً.

identity.name: SDL_SetAppMetadataProperty
identity.titleArabic: Specify metadata about your app through a set of properties

summary.actualTranslationArabic: Specify metadata about your app through a set of properties
summary.actualMeaningArabic: تمثل هذه الدالة عملية تشغيل SDL Set App Metadata الخاصية داخل SDL3، وتنفذ فعلاً عند استدعائها لا مجرد تعريف نظري.
summary.purposeArabic: تستخدم هذه الدالة عندما تحتاج إلى تشغيل SDL Set App Metadata الخاصية داخل التطبيق أو داخل مسار التنفيذ المرتبط بها.
summary.whyUseArabic: القيمة الحقيقية هنا هي الأثر التنفيذي الذي يحدثه الاستدعاء أو النتيجة التي يعيدها إلى التطبيق.
summary.carriedValueArabic: القيمة العملية لهذا الاستدعاء تظهر في ناتجه يعيد true عند النجاح وfalse عند الفشل؛ استخدم SDL_GetError لمعرفة السبب. وفي التغيير الذي يحدثه على المورد أو الحالة المرتبطة به.
summary.sourceDescription: Specify metadata about your app through a set of properties.

parameters.name: name
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل name من النوع const char * القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.
parameters.originalDescription: the name of the metadata property to set.

parameters.name: value
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل value من النوع const char * القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.
parameters.originalDescription: the value of the property, or NULL to remove that property.

remarks:
You can optionally provide metadata about your app to SDL. This is
not required, but strongly encouraged.

There are several locations where SDL can make use of metadata (an
"About" box in the macOS menu bar, the name of the app can be shown on
some audio mixers, etc). Any piece of metadata can be left out, if a
specific detail doesn't make sense for the app.

This function should be called as early as possible, before SDL_Init . Multiple calls to this function are
allowed, but various state might not change once it has been set up with
a previous call to this function.
Once set, this metadata can be read using SDL_GetAppMetadataProperty ().

SDL_PROP_APP_METADATA_NAME_STRING :
The human-readable name of the application, like "My Game 2: Bad Guy's
Revenge!". This will show up anywhere the OS shows the name of the
application separately from window titles, such as volume control
applets, etc. This defaults to "SDL Application".

SDL_PROP_APP_METADATA_VERSION_STRING :
The version of the app that is running; there are no rules on format, so
"1.0.3beta2" and "April 22nd, 2024" and a git hash are all valid
options. This has no default.

SDL_PROP_APP_METADATA_IDENTIFIER_STRING :
A unique string that identifies this app. This must be in reverse-domain
format, like "com.example.mygame2". This string is used by desktop
compositors to identify and group windows together, as well as match
applications with associated desktop settings and icons. If you plan to
package your application in a container such as Flatpak, the app ID
should match the name of your Flatpak container as well. This has no
default.

SDL_PROP_APP_METADATA_CREATOR_STRING :
The human-readable name of the creator/developer/maker of this app, like
"MojoWorkshop, LLC"

SDL_PROP_APP_METADATA_COPYRIGHT_STRING :
The human-readable copyright notice, like "Copyright (c) 2024
MojoWorkshop, LLC" or whatnot. Keep this to one line, don't paste a copy
of a whole software license in here. This has no default.

SDL_PROP_APP_METADATA_URL_STRING :
A URL to the app on the web. Maybe a product page, or a storefront, or
even a GitHub repository, for user's further information This has no
default.

SDL_PROP_APP_METADATA_TYPE_STRING :
The type of application this is. Currently this string can be "game" for
a video game, "mediaplayer" for a media player, or generically
"application" if nothing else applies. Future versions of SDL might add
new types. This defaults to "application".
details.returns: يعيد true عند النجاح وfalse عند الفشل؛ استخدم SDL_GetError لمعرفة السبب.
details.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
